concerns.text: Phase 2 tasks (T004-T007) are marked [P] (parallel-safe), but they have strict sequential data-flow dependencies: T004 (search) must complete before T005 (validate) can run, and T005's output (manifest vs. gap report) determines whether T006 (revise) or T007 (create manifest) executes. Marking these as parallel-safe violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule and creates a race condition where validation runs before search completes.

T008 (filtering utility) and T009 (env config) are marked [P], but T008 depends on the existence of `data/manifest.json` (produced by T007 in Phase 2) to know which files to filter. If Phase 2 fails, T008 has no input artifact. The [P] tag implies independence from Phase 2 completion, which is false; these tasks are consumers of Phase 2's output.

T012 (ingest) and T013 (extract) are marked [P], but T013 explicitly consumes the output of T012 (downloaded BAM/bigwig files). T013 cannot run in parallel with T012 as it requires the artifacts T012 produces. This is a direct 'Consumer before Producer' violation within the same user story block.

T020 (predictor) and T021 (train) are marked [P], but T021 requires the model architecture defined in T020 to be implemented before training can begin. While T020 defines the class, T021 instantiates and trains it; they are not independent. Furthermore, T025 (synthetic test) depends on the trained model from T024, yet T025 is marked [P] alongside T020-T024, implying it could run before the model is trained.

T028 (SAE) and T029 (validate) are marked [P], but T029 requires the latent features produced by T028 to perform correlation analysis. T029 cannot execute in parallel with T028. Similarly, T032 (save manifest) depends on the output of T029 and T030, yet is marked [P] with them.

T034-T040 are marked [P], but T040 (full pipeline integration test) explicitly depends on the completion of all previous phases (US1, US2, US3). It cannot run in parallel with the implementation of US1-3. While T034-T039 might be parallelizable among themselves, grouping T040 with them under [P] is misleading.
